appraisal_events.evidence: « la nature de l'illusion dont Robert de Saint-Loup était victime à l'égard de Rachel »; « elle continuait à régir ses actes comme ces astres qui nous gouvernent »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator frames Robert as deceived by a distance-enhanced image of Rachel and governed by desire, which drives him to seek introduction despite disenchanting details seen up close.

status_effects.explanation: He is locally weakened by being portrayed as emotionally governed by an illusory ideal of Rachel.
